scope: Engine-light persistence contracts and a Unity local-file adapter

sources.license_or_terms: Unity documentation terms; facts only, no copied implementation
admitted_claims: persistentDataPath is the Unity-provided per-application location for data retained between runs
the location and support behavior vary by platform and tvOS returns an empty path
a stable bundle identifier is material to retaining the same mobile path across updates
excluded_uses: No durability, atomicity, privacy, backup, cloud, or security guarantee is inferred
No platform is declared supported without exact-consumer evidence

sources.license_or_terms: Unity documentation terms; facts only, no copied implementation
admitted_claims: JsonUtility converts custom object DTOs to and from JSON using Unity serialization rules
top-level primitive and collection payloads require wrapper DTOs
a codec is a replaceable adapter rather than the persistence architecture
excluded_uses: JsonUtility does not define schema migration, integrity, atomic commit, or storage recovery
Live UnityEngine.Object graphs are not admitted as save-domain snapshots

sources.license_or_terms: Unity documentation terms; facts only, no copied implementation
admitted_claims: Unity serializes fields under Unity-specific supported-type rules
nested containers, dictionaries, object identity, cycles and polymorphism require explicit handling
authored Unity assets and runtime save state require an explicit conversion boundary
excluded_uses: Unity serialization support is not a reason to persist arbitrary scene or asset objects

sources.license_or_terms: Microsoft documentation terms; API contract only
admitted_claims: File.Replace replaces one file with another and can create a backup of the replaced file
the operation has explicit platform and exception boundaries that a provider must expose
excluded_uses: Availability on one runtime does not prove availability or crash durability on every Unity target
Fallback copy/delete/move sequences are not relabeled atomic

sources.license_or_terms: Microsoft documentation terms; API contract only
admitted_claims: FileStream exposes buffered flush behavior and a flush-to-disk overload on supported runtimes
provider results must distinguish serialization, write, flush and commit failures
excluded_uses: A successful flush is not a universal power-loss or filesystem guarantee

sources.license_or_terms: Microsoft documentation terms; API contract only
admitted_claims: SHA-256 produces a fixed-size digest suitable for detecting payload changes
excluded_uses: An unkeyed checksum does not authenticate a save or provide encryption/tamper resistance

sources.maintenance_evidence: 663 GitHub stars at review; tests present; latest reviewed commit dated 2025-10-06; Unity 6 compatibility documented
sources.admitted_role: Professional ecosystem evidence for replaceable serializers, path resolution, async APIs and tests
admitted_claims: A Unity save library benefits from explicit serializer and path seams
Cross-platform behavior and asynchronous operations require dedicated adapters and tests
excluded_uses: Its direct File.WriteAllText/File.Create path is not admitted as atomic-write or recovery evidence
Its encoding option is not admitted as a security standard
Its static facade is not imposed on the XR Foundry architecture

context_only_not_derivation_inputs.reason: Official and popular Apache-2.0 sample, but explicitly legacy since 2021 and its direct write helper does not cover migrations, integrity, atomic commit or recovery
Useful navigation and coverage vocabulary, but its reference section is pending and it is not an external authoritative derivation source

source_gate_non_claims: No source grants XR Foundry maturity, release, package admission or device authority
No source proves cloud, console, WebGL, mobile, XR device, security or crash-durability behavior for the future implementation
Popularity is corroborating adoption evidence only and never substitutes for scoped architecture and test review
